person.name: Konrad Rzentarzewski
person.bio: I was there when the config burned. In the age before observability, chaos reigned. The servers howled, and nothing scaled but entropy. Then kube cattle were born — simple, nameless beasts born of children’s games. But they multiplied. And their hunger grew vast. Years passed. The sun revolved the Earth. Then the Earth revolved the Cloud. I grew tired of debugging in darkness. I longed for light. And logs. Preferably structured. That’s when I began preaching — not loudly, just… declaratively. One apply at a time. I walked among DevOps tribes, where bash begat shell, and shell begat pain. To them I brought ConfigMaps, and Secrets hidden in plain sight. Then came the mysterious YAML — a language too holy to lint — inscribed deep within the API’s own heart. Through toil and merge, the infrastructure became manifest. Pods floated on the waters. Deployments begat replicas. Helm bore Charts. Git begat Ops. What was once dev spun to prod. And every service had a name — and every name resolved. Unless external DNS was involved. And the Preachers said: \"Behold, our logs streameth clean.\" Thus began the age of the declarative. We once dwelled in darkness. Now, we scale planetary in full light.

person.name: Lee Calcote
person.bio:

Previously, Calcote stewarded technology strategy and innovation across SolarWinds. He led software-defined data center engineering at Seagate, delivering predictive analytics and modern systems management. Calcote held various leadership positions at Cisco, where he created Cisco’s cloud management platforms and pioneered new, automated, remote management services.
In addition to his role at Layer5, Calcote serves in various industry bodies, including as a SIG chair in Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), and formerly, participated in the delivery of Redfish 1.0 in the Distributed Management Task Foundation (DMTF), and Docker Benchmark 1.0 in Center for Internet Security (CIS). He is a startup advisor, Docker Captain, Cloud Native Ambassador, O’Reilly author, and speaker in the cloud native ecosystem.
Calcote is a maintainer of open source projects, including Meshery, Service Mesh Interface, and Service Mesh Performance. Calcote holds a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, a master’s degree in Business Administration from California State University, Fresno and retains a list of industry certifications.

He strives to share his knowledge with others by providing presentations, workshops, and training. He creates a variety of content starting, from articles, ebooks, courses published on his website (https://cloudowski.com), to videos on his YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@Cloudowski) and the “Rozchmurzony” Podcast (in Polish). He despises raw onions and dishonesty. Loves authenticity and relationships based on it, and a good cheesecake.

person.name: Akitsugu Kitade
person.bio: I am an engineer specializing in building and operating OpenShift clusters. My expertise lies in CNCF technologies such as Kubernetes, container orchestration, and cloud-native infrastructure. I focus on automation, scalability, and reliability to help organizations adopt cloud-native solutions effectively.

person.name: Atulpriya Sharma
person.bio: I'm a tester turned developer advocate. I work on & talk about Cloud native, Kubernetes & DevOps. I also create content - blog posts, Twitter threads - & host Twitter spaces. In addition, I'm one of the organiser of CNCF Hyderabad and strongly believe in collaborative learning and growth. When I am not working, I'm a food & travel blogger & love exploring eateries & going on road trips. You can find me at @TheTechMaharaj on Twitter.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am absolutely stoked to be a CNCF ambassador! Being a part of this community means I get to geek out with a group of like-minded individuals who are passionate about Cloud native technologies, Kubernetes, and DevOps. It's an opportunity to keep learning and growing, while also sharing my knowledge and enthusiasm with people in Hyderabad. I'm excited to dive headfirst into the latest trends and developments, all while having a blast! I can't wait to host fun events, share cool content, and help spread the word about CNCF and related technologies. Overall, this is not just a chance to contribute to a thriving ecosystem, but to also make connections, learn new things, and have a ton of fun along the way!

person.name: Bhushan Rane
person.bio: Bhushan Rane is a DevOps, Platform Engineering, and Data specialist with hands-on experience across multi-cloud environments. He focuses on Kubernetes, ArgoCD, DevSecOps, and observability within CNCF ecosystems. Passionate about AI-driven automation and self-hosted infrastructure, he regularly experiments and shares learnings from his homelab projects. He is active in cloud-native communities and enjoys translating complex systems into practical solutions.

person.name: Seito Miyazaki
person.bio: Seito Miyazaki is a Software Engineer at Hitachi, Ltd. He is a team lead of developing managed services of container platform and has assisted enterprise customers to containerize applications and build CI/CD pipelines. He is experienced in public cloud(AWS), Infrastructure as Code (Ansible), GitOps, Kubernetes, and other related cloud-native technologies. He also holds several advanced certifications of public cloud.

person.name: Subash P
person.bio: I'm a Cloud and DevOps Architect with extensive experience in debugging and troubleshooting. Very keen on learning new things. Kubernetes always makes me curious due to it's elasticity which made to acquire Kubeastronaut.

person.name: Jeffrey Chin
person.bio: As a lead engineer and DevOps enthusiast, I’ve consistently demonstrated a proactive approach, taking ownership of projects and delivering with excellence. My commitment to mentoring peers reflects my passion for knowledge sharing and growth. Beyond work, I find joy in maintaining my Kubernetes-based home lab and creating memorable moments with my wife and kids.

person.name: Radosław Kordula
person.bio: Engineer focused on distributed systems, networking and automation. Enjoys learning, building and sharing knowledge.

person.name: Rishabh Vishwakarma
person.bio: I am Rishabh Vishwakarma, a DevOps Engineer with over nine years of experience leading the design and operation of cloud-native platforms in enterprise environments. I partner closely with engineering, architecture, and infrastructure teams to drive Kubernetes adoption, optimize delivery pipelines, and build scalable, resilient systems aligned with CNCF best practices. My expertise spans Kubernetes and the CNCF ecosystem, Microsoft Azure, Azure DevOps, infrastructure automation with Terraform, and robust backup and disaster recovery solutions.

person.name: Thiago Sinji Shimada Ramos
person.bio: I'm Thiago Ramos, a BizDevOps professional passionate about cloud. I co-host Kubernetes User Groups (K8SUG) and run Cloud Native Melbourne, I am also part of CNCF Security Technical Advisory Group APAC. My international background—from Brazil to Japan and now Australia—has shaped my collaborative and innovative approach to technology. Outside of tech, I enjoy playing Go and solving magic cubes, hobbies that sharpen my strategic thinking.

person.name: Wael Ayadi
person.bio: I am a cloud and DevOps consultant with 10 years of experience in the IT industry. I began my career as a system administrator before transitioning into the world of cloud and DevOps. Over the years, I have tackled numerous challenges by participating in IT projects and honing my skills in technologies such as AWS, Terraform, Kubernetes, Helm, ArgoCD, and many others. A big fan of CNCF solutions, I continuously strive to stay at the forefront of innovations in this field.

person.name: Chu-Chun Chang
person.bio: Enjoy exploring cloud native technologies, Interested in Kubernetes, and GitOps tools like ArgoCD...etc. I love to work with organizations together with team's success.  Outside work, I love to explore the world.

person.name: Fevzi Sahinler
person.bio: Fevzi works as a DevOps and Platform Engineer, mostly dealing with Kubernetes-based platforms in production. He spends his time designing, operating, and improving cloud-native infrastructure using tools from the CNCF ecosystem.

person.name: Julius Da Silva Maia
person.bio: Julius Maia is a Technical Account Manager at AWS focused on strategic Media and Entertainment customers and is passionate about containers and DevOps. With proven skills in resolving complex issues, implementing improvements, and collaborating with diverse stakeholders. Certified in Kubernetes, AWS, and Cisco, with a strong track record in analytical problem-solving and effective communication.

person.name: Anuhya Polisetti
person.bio: Attention is all you need is the approach i take while tackling every situation I face with. While a firm believer of "Nature isn't classical" , navigating the complexity of "now()" with a deeper sense of science, spirituality and scale.

person.name: Christopher Duffy
person.bio: Chris, or know to his friends and coworkers as Duffy, is a dedicated architect who helps businesses seamlessly migrate and optimize their operations in the cloud. Passionate about CNCF technologies, CI/CD, containerization, and orchestration, he leverages these tools to transform systems for increased scalability and agility. A lifelong learner, Duffy constantly experiments with and adopts cutting-edge technologies, ensuring his clients' cloud journeys remain future-proof.

person.name: Christopher Hanson
person.bio: I have taught hundreds of DevOps engineers at Fortune 100 companies how to successfully use Kubernetes, Docker, OpenShift, Prometheus, Istio, Spinnaker and other cloud-native tooling to package, deploy, and orchestrate microservice applications at scale. I give frequent talks at industry conferences to share my expertise with the community and write technical blogs with the RX-M team at https://rx-m.com/blog/.
person.company: RX-M Cloud Native & AI Consulting and Training

person.name: Kunchala Vikram Babu
person.bio: As a Senior Technical Specialist at Sony India Software Centre, I leverage my expertise in DevOps, cloud technologies, Python, Go, and Linux to design, develop, and deliver cutting-edge cloud-native applications and solutions for Sony's diverse product portfolio. My proficiency in Kubernetes, Terraform, and other cloud platforms enables me to automate, orchestrate, and secure the deployment and management of containerized workloads across various environments.  I am driven by a passion for continuous learning, problem-solving, and knowledge sharing. This dedication is reflected in my role at Sony and through my YouTube channel, DevOps Made Easy, where I share tutorials, tips, and best practices on a range of DevOps topics. With over 20,000 subscribers, my channel has become a valuable resource for the DevOps community, earning widespread positive feedback and engagement.

person.name: Kevin Mcclaren
person.bio: Site Reliability Engineer and Consultant, with extensive experience across the Cloud Native landscape, from Kubernetes and platform security to chaos engineering at scale. I focus on building reliable, observable, and secure systems through Infrastructure as Code, AWS, and disciplined incident response in highly complex environments. Outside of SRE work I am a passionate ultrarunner/rock climber who finds balance in nature.

person.name: Saleh Alshamlan
person.bio: A Software Engineer that is passionate about DevOps, Software Architecture & Design and Cloud Platforms & Technologies. Along with a major interest and multiple years of experience in Kubernetes and Containerization.

person.name: Aung Aung Myo Myint
person.bio: I'm a Senior Server and Cloud Specialist advocate with linux and cloud-native technologies. I am keep learning Cloud-Native Computing Kubernetes, Prometheus, and other CNCF projects.  My expertise lies in linux, AWS Cloud, Redhat Openstack, container orchestration and microservices architecture.

person.name: Nikhil Nischal
person.bio: An Engineer and Kubernetes enthusiast, committed to mastering cloud‑native technologies and driving innovation in container orchestration - backed by 20+ certifications validating expertise in Kubernetes, Docker, Linux, and cloud platforms.

person.name: Servé Laurijssen
person.bio: Developer  Working with rancher and k8s on VMware on a daily basis Loves modernizing deprecated code

person.name: Mohamed Ahmed Abdelaziz
person.bio: I am a Senior DevOps Engineer with  expertise in cloud-native technologies and a strong focus on the CNCF ecosystem. I design and manage scalable, resilient infrastructure using Kubernetes, and other CNCF projects. Passionate about automation and GitOps, I help organizations streamline deployments and enhance system reliability. I'm committed to continuous learning and contributing to the cloud-native community. I enjoy contributing to the CNCF community and collaborating with like-minded peers to advance open-source innovation.

person.name: Andrew Nelson
person.bio: Andrew Nelson is a security leader specializing in vulnerability management and Kubernetes and container security. He recently presented at BSides Chicago 2025 on moving beyond CVSS-based vulnerability prioritization toward risk-based approaches. Andrew develops open-source security tools that integrate threat intelligence sources like EPSS and CISA KEV data to help teams make smarter remediation decisions. Find his projects at github.com/nelssec.

person.name: Bounouh Fadi
person.bio: I am Fedi Bounouh, a cloud engineer with expertise in DevOps and cloud-native technologies, including AWS, Google Cloud, and Kubernetes. I hold multiple certifications across CNCF and cloud platforms, specializing in CI/CD automation, containerization, and infrastructure management using tools like Terraform and Kubernetes.

person.name: Gnani Rahul Nutakki
person.bio: I am a Principal Cloud Platform Engineer at Radiant Logic with over eight years of experience in cloud automation, Kubernetes, Terraform, and modern infrastructure design. I hold multiple Kubernetes and AWS certifications, focusing on building secure, scalable, and efficient platform systems across multi-cloud environments. My work addresses critical challenges in agentic AI security, discovery, and management. I am also passionate about cloud-native technologies and continuous learning, regularly sharing insights through on my Medium publication Clear-Text.

person.name: Johnnatan Malpica
person.bio: I am a passionate Cloud Engineer and long-time FLOSS advocate who keeps learning a little more about CNCF and other technologies each day. I believe that success stories revolve around collaboration, ethics, inclusion, and positive thinking.

person.name: Jonathan Bryce
person.bio: Jonathan Bryce is the Executive Director of Cloud & Infrastructure at the Linux Foundation, where he leads both the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) and the OpenInfra Foundation—two of the largest and most influential open source communities in the world. With over 15 years of open source leadership, Jonathan co-founded the OpenStack project, guiding its evolution into a global ecosystem that supports a multi-billion-dollar market and serves as the de facto standard for open cloud infrastructure. Before his open source career, Jonathan spent over a decade in data center infrastructure and co-founded one of the industry's earliest cloud computing companies, which grew into a billion-dollar business following its acquisition by Rackspace. Passionate about open collaboration, infrastructure innovation, and empowering users, contributors, and ecosystems worldwide, Jonathan frequently speaks at international conferences on open source strategy, infrastructure modernization, cloud-native technologies, and open source AI initiatives.

person.name: Joël Van Der Voort Van Der Kleij
person.bio: Passionate Linux and open source advocate with a strong track record in operations, consulting, and infrastructure design. As a seasoned multidisciplinary engineer, I specialize in cloud-native architecture at ISP scale—building resilient, scalable platforms that power modern services.

person.name: Lucas Stofaleti
person.bio: I'm a Senior DevOps Engineer with a passion for continuous delivery and infrastructure automation. As a big advocate for open-source technology, I believe in using technology to bridge the gap between people and companies.

person.name: Robert Maxwell
person.bio: A problem solver since youth, taking apart computers to understand how they work, to graduating in computer science, and working for many years in different industries. First became curious around containerisation with Cloud Foundry and then moved onto Kubernetes and its whole ecosystem. Now works to help developers gain faster feedback on their work and ensure workloads run securely and efficiently.

person.name: Jade Lassery
person.bio: I’m a Cloud Native specialist and open source advocate with a background in Physics and Computer Science. My work sits at the intersection of technology and human impact, connecting people, nice tools, and communities to drive real outcomes. With a strong background in open source technologies, open communities, and modern cloud infrastructure. My expertise spans Kubernetes, Containers, DevOps, observability, SaaS/PaaS platforms, and application modernization. You’ll often find me at KubeCon, Open Source Summit, and CNCF meetups. Originally from Brazil and now based in Europe, I bring a global mindset and a passion for fostering collaboration and building strong technical communities.

person.name: Roy Liang
person.bio: I am a cloud native developer and advocator with over 6 years of experience in cloud native community. I was a cloud native infrastructure SRE and early Kubernetes contributor. I am currently working as a Senior R&D Engineer in Machine Learning Platform team at Tencent. I have participated in multiple cloud native projects and now I am working on cloud native AI infrastructure. I am also a co-author of "Istio Handbook", traslator of "Distributed Machine Learning Pattern" and speaker of KubeCon.

person.name: Sairam Madasu
person.bio: I'm a cloud engineer at Microsoft. I'm also a independent researcher with my area expertise in cloud and AI. I published many IEEE articles at International IEEE conferences.
